---
title-meta: "PolicyEngine UK: a tax-benefit microsimulation model, v1"
author-meta: "Max Ghenis"
date: ""
bibliography: bibliography/references.bib
format:
  html:
    cite-method: citeproc
    link-citations: true
    link-bibliography: true
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    classoption: 11pt
    pdf-engine: pdflatex
    cite-method: natbib
    biblio-style: plainnat
    include-in-header:
      - preamble.tex
    keep-tex: true
number-sections: true
---

<!-- v1 outline (PolicyEngine/policyengine-uk-paper#1). Section files are
stubs: each one states what the section will claim and cites the evidence
that already exists for it, or is marked \todo{} where no such evidence has
been found yet. No results, figures, or numbers in this PR are fabricated;
every number that appears is copied from a cited, committed source. Author
list, order, and affiliations are TBD -- see disclosures.tex. -->

\title{PolicyEngine UK: a tax-benefit microsimulation model, v1}

\author{
    Max Ghenis\thanks{PolicyEngine. Corresponding author: max@policyengine.org}
}
\date{}

\maketitle

\input{sections/abstract}
\input{sections/introduction}
\input{sections/model}
\input{sections/data}
\input{sections/validation}
\input{sections/discussion}
\input{sections/conclusion}
\input{sections/disclosures}
